The Institution offers educational programs (“ Programs ”) for students (“ Students ”) in physical therapy, exercise science, and/or related health fields at various locations (each, a “ Facility ”). Programs include unpaid, supervised clinical experiences at a Facility. Exos will collaborate with the Institution to provide Programs aligning with the Institution's educational objectives.

our_standard.text:
Overview
Institution Responsibilities | Exos Responsibilities | Joint Responsibilities
- Provide information reasonably requested about Students to enable Exos to evaluate their candidacy. - Ensure Students are prepared for the Program by qualified faculty, with measured academic performance. - Coordinator to manage Students’ educational experiences for the Program. - Maintain accreditations/ licenses required of an educational institution. | - Determine which Students may participate in the Program and assign them to a Facility. - Offer Students clinical experiences meeting accreditation standards. - Appoint “Facility Coordinator” to oversee clinical experience and enable Student activities based on the agreed objectives. - Orient Students to the Program and applicable Exos rules and policies. - Maintain qualifications/ licenses required to provide clinical experiences to Students. - Promptly notify Institution of any student injuries, and arrange medical care per its policies, but without financial responsibility. | - Meet periodically to review key Program objectives. - Cooperate to supervise and evaluate Students, including providing feedback on Student’s performance throughout the Program.

preferred_variations.to: 2.3 Site  Coordinator. The Facility Coordinator shall be responsible for overall management of the experience at Exos.

preferred_variations.to: 3.2 Review and Evaluation of Affiliation. This Agreement may be amended or modified, pursuant to Section 7 below, to reflect changes in the parties' relationship.

our_standard.text: 1.8. Term. Unless otherwise specified in an agreement referencing these Terms, this Agreement is effective as of the Effective Date for an initial term of five years. Thereafter, it will automatically renew for successive one-year periods, unless either party provides at least 60 days’ prior written notice of non-renewal, to be effective as of the end of the then-current term; provided, however, that non-renewal shall not disrupt the clinical experience of any Student actively serving in the Program, and the Agreement shall remain effective as to such Student(s) until the end of their clinical experience. Following non-renewal by either party, no new Students will be placed at any Facility.

our_standard.text: 1.9.2. For Cause. This Agreement may be terminated for cause as follows:

preferred_variations.if:
B. For Cause. In the event the Institution or Exos fails in any substantial manner to perform as required herein, this Agreement may be terminated as described below:
Either  party  may  terminate  th
preferred_variations.to:
B. For Cause. In the event the Institution or Exos fails in any substantial manner to perform as required herein, this Agreement may be terminated as described below:
Either  party  may  terminate  this  Agreement  at  any  time,  upon  material  breach  of  any  of  its provisions by the other party; provided, however, that not less than thirty (30) days prior to termination, written notice shall be given by the non-breaching party to the breaching party that states the intention of the non-breaching party to terminate this Agreement, the nature of the material  breach  giving  rise  to  termination  and  shall  permit  the  breaching  party  reasonable opportunity to cure such material breach during said thirty (30) day period (" Cure Period ").
If the material breach is not resolved to the satisfaction of the non-breaching party during the Cure Period as provided in Section 6.2 (B)(1) above, the non-breaching party may immediately terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the breaching party.
In the event that termination of the Agreement by Exos pursuant to this Section 6.2 (B) disrupts the clinical experience of any Student(s) in the Program, the parties shall attempt, in good faith and using their best efforts, to continue Students' clinical experiences, and this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until such time as this Agreement may expire without disruption of said Students' clinical experience. During any time period in which notice of termination has been given and existing Students are completing the Program, no new Student may be placed at any Exos facility.

preferred_variations.if:
B. For Cause. This Agreement may be terminated for cause as follows:
By either party, upon the other party's breach of this Agreement; provided the non-breaching party provides at least 30 days' notic
preferred_variations.to:
B. For Cause. This Agreement may be terminated for cause as follows:
By either party, upon the other party's breach of this Agreement; provided the non-breaching party provides at least 30 days' notice and opportunity to cure (if such breach is capable of cure, and otherwise such termination shall be immediately effective).
By either party upon notice, if the other party fails to maintain such accreditation as is required for Students to receive academic credit, or the intended clinical experience, as applicable.

preferred_variations.if:
B. For Cause. In the event the Institution or the Facility fails in any substantial manner to perform as required herein, this Agreement may be terminated as described below:
(1) Either  party  may  t
preferred_variations.to:
B. For Cause. In the event the Institution or the Facility fails in any substantial manner to perform as required herein, this Agreement may be terminated as described below:
(1) Either  party  may  terminate  this  Agreement  at  any  time,  upon material breach of any of its provisions by the other party; provided, however, that not less than thirty (30) days prior to termination, written notice shall be given by the non-breaching party to the breaching party that states the intention of the non-breaching party to terminate this Agreement, the nature of the material breach giving rise to termination and shall permit the breaching party reasonable opportunity to cure such material breach during said thirty (30) day period (" Cure Period ").
(2) If the material breach is not resolved to the satisfaction of the nonbreaching party during the Cure Period as provided in Section 6.2 (B)(1) above, the non-breaching party may immediately terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the breaching party.
(3) In  the  event  that  termination  of  the  Agreement  by  the  Facility pursuant to this Section 6.2 (B) disrupts the clinical experience of any Student(s) in the Program, the parties shall attempt, in good faith and using their best efforts, to continue Students' clinical experiences,  and  this  Agreement  shall  remain  in  full  force  and  effect  until  such  time  as  this Agreement may expire without disruption of said Students' clinical experience. During any time period in which notice of termination has been given and existing Students are completing the Program, no new Student may be placed at the Facility.

B. For Cause.  In the event the Institution or the Facility fails in any substantial manner to perform as required herein, this Agreement may be terminated as described below:
(1) Either  party  may
preferred_variations.to:
B. For Cause.  In the event the Institution or the Facility fails in any substantial manner to perform as required herein, this Agreement may be terminated as described below:
(1) Either  party  may  terminate  this  Agreement  at  any  time,  upon material breach of any of its provisions by the other party; provided, however, that not less than thirty (30) days prior to termination, written notice shall be given by the non-breaching party to the breaching party that states the intention of the non-breaching party to terminate this Agreement, the nature of the material breach giving rise to termination and shall permit the breaching party reasonable opportunity to cure such material breach during said thirty (30) day period (" Cure Period ").
(2) If the material breach is not resolved to the satisfaction of the nonbreaching party during the Cure Period as provided in Section 6.2 (B)(1) above, the non-breaching party may immediately terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the breaching party.
(3) In  the  event  that  termination  of  the  Agreement  by  the  Facility pursuant to this Section 6.2 (B) disrupts the clinical experience of any Student(s) in the Program, the parties shall attempt, in good faith and using their best efforts, to continue Students' clinical experiences,  and  this  Agreement  shall  remain  in  full  force  and  effect  until  such  time  as  this Agreement may expire without disruption of said Students' clinical experience.  During any time period in which notice of termination has been given and existing Students are completing the Program, no new Student may be placed at the Facility.
